233	
234	- **`Functor.preservesFiniteLimits_iff_forall_exact_map_and_mono` packages SES exactness preservation from LEFT-half limit preservation only** *(NEW iter-195 Archon canonical — Lane H `Scheme.IsFlasque.shortExact_app_surjective` closure in `RiemannRoch/H1Vanishing.lean`, axiom-clean. Closes the iter-192/193/194 STUCK on `SAb.Exact`.)* For proving `(S.map F).Exact` given `S : ShortComplex C` is `ShortExact` and `F : C ⥤ D` is additive between abelian categories, you do NOT need full `Functor.PreservesHomology` (i.e. BOTH `PreservesFiniteLimits` AND `PreservesFiniteColimits` + `Exact`-preservation). Mathlib's `Functor.preservesFiniteLimits_iff_forall_exact_map_and_mono` states `PreservesFiniteLimits F ↔ ∀ S, S.ShortExact → (S.map F).Exact ∧ Mono (F.map S.f)`. The `.1` projection delivers exactness directly from LEFT-half limit preservation. **Iter-195 instance**: closed Lane H final residual via 1 new project-local instance `sheafCompose_preservesFiniteLimits` (~30 LOC, see next entry) instead of the plan-anticipated ~50-100 LOC `PreservesHomology` chain. Reusable for ANY "SES is preserved by a functor between abelian categories" obligation when the inputs are known to be `ShortExact`. **Anti-pattern**: do NOT chase the full `PreservesHomology` substrate when the `ShortExact` packaging is available — the LEFT-half iff is qualitatively cheaper.

236	- **`sheafCompose_preservesFiniteLimits` (factor through faithful-and-reflects `sheafToPresheaf`)** *(NEW iter-195 Archon canonical — Lane H project-local instance in `RiemannRoch/H1Vanishing.lean` (~L340), axiom-clean.)* For proving `PreservesFiniteLimits (sheafCompose J F)` given `F : A ⥤ B` between abelian categories with finite limits, `PreservesFiniteLimits F`, and `J.HasSheafCompose F`: factor via the rfl-equality `sheafCompose J F ⋙ sheafToPresheaf J B = sheafToPresheaf J A ⋙ (whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj F` (by `rfl`); get `PreservesFiniteLimits` on the RHS via composing `whiskeringRight_preservesLimitsOfShape` (Mathlib `CategoryTheory.Limits.Preserves.FunctorCategory`) with `sheafToPresheaf` finite-limit-preservation (Mathlib `CategoryTheory.Sites.Limits`); transport back via `preservesFiniteLimits_of_reflects_of_preserves` (using `sheafToPresheaf J B` fully faithful ⟹ reflects isomorphisms). ~30 LOC. Reusable for ANY `sheafCompose`-derived preservation argument when the source `F` is a finite-limit-preserving additive functor (e.g. `forget₂ ModuleCat AddCommGrpCat`).

Reusable for ANY "extract a vector outside a span" argument from a finrank gap (Hartshorne II.6.7-style genus-0 base-change arguments + RR-induction).

242	- **Structural lift via file-private named typed-sorry helper preserves no-regression on sorry count when substrate-blocked** *(NEW iter-195 Archon canonical — Lane RCI `localParameterAtInfty_uniformiser_witness` in `RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean:463`, kernel-only `[propext, sorryAx, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`.)* When an inline `?hyp` typed sorry inside a consumer body is genuinely substrate-blocked (witness construction requires multi-step Mathlib gap fill), the canonical discipline is a 1:1 swap to a file-private typed-sorry helper with documented closure path. Properties of the structural lift: (a) net sorry count unchanged (1:1 swap inline → named); (b) consumer body becomes sorry-free at the body level (delegates via `exact <helper> <args>`); (c) substrate need exposed at a single named declaration with a focused iter-(N+1) target + documented recipe; (d) tactic experiments at the consumer (`Classical.arbitrary`, `tauto`, `exact?`) can be definitively closed-out as dead — the helper docstring records what was tried. **Anti-pattern**: do NOT revert the upstream signature refactor (e.g. iter-194 `lane-i-localparameter-signature-v2`) when its `?hyp` is substrate-blocked — the structural lift preserves the stronger signature for the next iter. Reusable for any "iter-(N-1) refactor introduced a stronger signature whose `?hyp` is inevitably substrate-blocked" pattern.

Neither committed any edits; ~25 min of substrate exploration was lost as working context. **Mitigation for plan agents**: high-cost lanes (substrate-scouting lanes; lanes whose closure cascade-unblocks 2+ downstream lanes) should carry an explicit "checkpoint commit" instruction: after ≥30 min of substrate scouting with no edit committed, commit a substrate-roadmap comment block to the file at the scaffold sorry sites before continuing. This preserves prover-session value on transient API failure. **Iter-196 implication**: re-dispatch the 2 errored lanes is mandatory — neither is a mathematical failure. **Reusable** for any project-side lane facing extended scouting before first edit.

246	- **`forget₂` bridge from `Sheaf J (ModuleCat kbar)` to `Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat` for module-valued sheaf SES surjectivity work** *(NEW iter-194 Archon canonical — Lane H `Scheme.IsFlasque.shortExact_app_surjective` body in `RiemannRoch/H1Vanishing.lean`, structural decomposition axiom-clean modulo a single typeclass goal `(sheafCompose forget₂).PreservesHomology`.)* When a SES of `Sheaf J (ModuleCat kbar)` needs surjectivity-on-sections proven (Hartshorne II Ex 1.16(b)), DO NOT fight the module-valued category. Lift to `Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat` via `sheafCompose (forget₂ ModuleCat AddCommGrpCat)`; apply Mathlib's `TopCat.Sheaf.IsFlasque.epi_of_shortExact` to get `Epi (SAb.g.hom.app (op U))`; convert to surjectivity via `AddCommGrpCat.epi_iff_surjective`; observe the underlying function is the same as the original `ModuleCat kbar`-level map. **Substrate helpers needed (~35 LOC total, axiom-clean)**: (a) `sheafCompose_additive` instance — `F : A ⥤ B` additive + `J.HasSheafCompose F` ⟹ `(sheafCompose J F).Additive`, factoring through `sheafToPresheaf J B`'s faithfulness + `Functor.whiskerRight` componentwise additivity; (b) `sheafCompose_preservesZero` instance derived via `Functor.preservesZeroMorphisms_of_additive`; (c) `Scheme.IsFlasque.toAddCommGrpCat` bridge — for each pair `V ≤ U`, the underlying restriction map is the same function after `forget₂`, so surjectivity (project predicate) ↔ Epi (AddCommGrpCat-level via `AddCommGrpCat.epi_iff_surjective`). Mathlib's `Functor.preservesFiniteLimits_iff_forall_exact_map_and_mono` states `PreservesFiniteLimits F ↔ ∀ S, S.ShortExact → (S.map F).Exact ∧ Mono (F.map S.f)`. The `.1` projection delivers exactness directly from LEFT-half limit preservation. **Iter-195 instance**: closed Lane H final residual via 1 new project-local instance `sheafCompose_preservesFiniteLimits` (~30 LOC, see next entry) instead of the plan-anticipated ~50-100 LOC `PreservesHomology` chain. Reusable for ANY "SES is preserved by a functor between abelian categories" obligation when the inputs are known to be `ShortExact`. **Anti-pattern**: do NOT chase the full `PreservesHomology` substrate when the `ShortExact` packaging is available — the LEFT-half iff is qualitatively cheaper.

### Proof Patterns (reusable across targets)

- **`Algebra.SubmersivePresentation` 0×0-Jacobian recipe for `MvPolynomial (Fin n)` standard-smooth** *(NEW iter-196 Archon canonical — Lane BareScheme substrate `mvPolynomialFin_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension` in `Genus0BaseObjects/BareScheme.lean:165-211`, ~50 LOC axiom-clean.)* Closes the iter-182-flagged Mathlib gap on `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)`. Construction: (1) `mvPolyGenerators` via `Algebra.Generators.ofSurjective val := X` + `MvPolynomial.aeval_X_left` for surjectivity; (2) `mvPolyPresentation` with `σ := PEmpty` (no relations, ker = ⊥ via `Algebra.Generators.ker_eq_ker_aeval_val`); (3) `mvPolyPreSubmersivePresentation` with empty `map : PEmpty → α`; (4) `mvPolySubmersivePresentation` with `dimension := Nat.card (Fin n) - Nat.card PEmpty = n` and Jacobian = det of 0×0 matrix = 1 via `Matrix.det_isEmpty`; (5) instance via `Algebra.SubmersivePresentation.isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension`. The recipe is pure Mathlib supplement and does not depend on project objects — usable wherever `MvPolynomial (Fin n) R` standard-smooth-of-relative-dimension-n is needed. **Mathlib upstream PR candidate**.

- **`IsZariskiLocalAtSource.of_openCover` cover-reduction for smoothness** *(NEW iter-196 Archon canonical — Lane BareScheme `projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim` in `Genus0BaseObjects/BareScheme.lean:325`, axiom-clean wrapper.)* For any morphism property `P` with `IsZariskiLocalAtSource P`, `P f` follows from `∀ i, P (𝒰.f i ≫ f)` for an open cover `𝒰 : Scheme.OpenCover X`. For `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n` (which has `HasRingHomProperty` ⟹ `IsZariskiLocalAtSource` per Mathlib `RingHomProperties.lean:375`), this delegates smoothness on `(ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left` to per-chart smoothness on the 2-chart affine cover. Reusable for ANY `SmoothOfRelativeDimension` / `Smooth` / `LocallyOfFiniteType` / `LocallyOfFinitePresentation` proof on a `Proj` scheme via the standard affine cover.

- **`ObjectProperty.FullSubcategory.ext` for lifting presheaf equalities to sheaf equalities** *(NEW iter-196 Archon canonical — Lane H `skyscraperSheaf_eq_pushforward_const` outer step in `RiemannRoch/H1Vanishing.lean:818`, axiom-clean (~10 LOC).)* When a Mathlib lemma proves `P_presheaf = Q_presheaf` for two presheaves and both are also sheaves with the SAME sheaf condition, the equality lifts to a sheaf-level **equality** (not just iso) via `ObjectProperty.FullSubcategory.ext`. The `IsSheaf` Prop-valued field is subsingleton-equal, so the only content of a sheaf equality is the presheaf equality. Pattern is reusable wherever a Mathlib `_eq_pushforward` / `_eq_skyscraper` / etc. presheaf identity has a sheafification analogue — typically saves the entire iso-construction round-trip.

- **`LocallyQuasiFinite.of_finite_preimage_singleton` as LQF reformulation pattern** *(NEW iter-196 Archon canonical — Lane RCI helper (a) body reformulation in `RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean:884-943`.)* When the abstract per-fibre LQF goal `LocallyQuasiFinite (φ.fiberToSpecResidueField x)` is stuck, reformulate via Mathlib's `LocallyQuasiFinite.of_finite_preimage_singleton` (`Morphisms/QuasiFinite.lean:295`). The new obligation is `∀ x : Y, (φ.base ⁻¹' {x}).Finite` (concrete topological preimage-finiteness) given `[LocallyOfFiniteType φ]`. The reformulation converts an opaque algebro-geometric obligation into a concrete topological one amenable to case-splits (generic point vs closed points). Companion derivation: `LocallyOfFiniteType φ` is often in-scope via `[IsProper φ]` + `IsProper.toLocallyOfFiniteType`. Reusable for ANY scheme-theoretic LQF reformulation where the topological preimage at each point is easier to bound than the per-fibre algebraic statement.

- **Dependent-motive issue on `Scheme.Hom.app f V` BLOCKS direct `rw [f_eq_g]`** *(NEW iter-196 Archon canonical — Lane E `Proj.awayι_app_basicOpen` blocker in `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`.)* `Scheme.Hom.app f V : Γ(Y, V) ⟶ Γ(X, f⁻¹ᵁ V)` has codomain depending on `f` via the preimage `f⁻¹ᵁ V`. Any attempt to `rw [f_eq_g]` or `conv_lhs => rw [...]` on a goal mentioning `f.app V` fails with "motive is not type correct" because the codomain type `Γ(X, f⁻¹ᵁ V)` changes when `f` changes shape. **Workaround**: build an `appTop`-level helper (or `app U` helper for the specific U), then chain through `Scheme.Hom.comp_app` + `Scheme.Opens.ι_app` to evaluate componentwise. Iter-196 Lane E established this for `Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm = Spec.map (basicOpenIsoAway _).inv ≫ (isAffineOpen_basicOpen _).fromSpec` — directly `rw`ing on it under `awayι.app(basicOpen 𝒜 f)` fails; the iter-197 prescription is `Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_inv_app_top` helper via `Scheme.inv_app` + `Proj.basicOpenToSpec_app_top` then chain. Reusable for ANY equation between `Scheme.Hom`s when downstream goals mention `.app` of the morphism.

- **`Iso.ext` repackaging on `(A ≪≫ B).inv` for clean equality between two `Spec.map ... ≫ ...` compositions** *(NEW iter-196 Archon canonical — Lane E `Proj.awayι_eq_specMap_fromSpec` in `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean:199`, axiom-clean.)* When proving `g₁ ≫ ι = g₂ ≫ ι` for two compositions through an open immersion `ι`, after `congr 1` the remaining `g₁ = g₂` may be an iso equation. If both sides factor as `(α ≪≫ β).inv` for some `α : X ≅ Z`, `β : Z ≅ Y`, repackage via `change _ = (α ≪≫ Spec.mapIso β.op).inv`; then `apply Iso.ext`; reduce to forward direction `Iso.hom = ...` which is typically `rfl` after Mathlib `Iso.trans_hom`, `Functor.mapIso_hom`, `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec_hom` simp-rewrites. Used iter-196 to bridge `Proj.awayι` and `IsAffineOpen.fromSpec` via `basicOpenIsoSpec.hom = basicOpenToSpec = toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map(awayToSection)`. Reusable for `Proj`-side / affine-open iso bridges that compose multiple Mathlib `_iso_hom` simp lemmas.

- **8-step Route 2 PID-transfer recipe for `IsRegularInCodimensionOne` on `Proj` via affine chart + Spec.stalkIso** *(NEW iter-196 Archon canonical — Lane I `isRegularInCodimOneProjectiveLineBar` in `RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean:750`, axiom-clean modulo 1 named topological-to-algebraic residual.)* For a Proj scheme covered by affine charts, prove `Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X` via per-prime-divisor DVR by: (1) select chart `i := 𝒰.idx Y.point`; (2) open-immersion stalk iso `(𝒰.f i).stalkMap y : ProjStalk(Y.point) ⟶ ChartStalk(y)` via `IsOpenImmersion.iff_isIso_stalkMap`; (3) `IsDomain` transport via `MulEquiv.isDomain _ hiso.symm.commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv.toMulEquiv`; (4) reduce goal to DVR on `Localization.AtPrime y.asIdeal` via `Spec.stalkIso`; (5) `IsDomain`/`IsPrincipalIdealRing`/`IsDedekindDomain` on chart ring `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 X_i` via iso chain `Away 𝒜 X_i ≃+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar ≃+* Polynomial kbar` + `Polynomial.instEuclideanDomain` + `EuclideanDomain.to_principal_ideal_domain` + `IsPrincipalIdealRing.isDedekindDomain`; (6) bridge `(![X 0, X 1]) i = X i` defeq via universal-quantifier `∀ j : Fin 2, ...` + `fin_cases` (defeq does NOT auto-elaborate); (7) `IsLocalization.AtPrime.isDiscreteValuationRing_of_dedekind_domain` with named residual `y.asIdeal ≠ ⊥` (Stacks 02IZ/005X coheight ↔ Krull-dim gap); (8) transport DVR back via `IsDiscreteValuationRing.RingEquivClass.isDiscreteValuationRing`. Total ~150 LOC; substantive structural advance reducing a vague PID-transfer-50-80-LOC sorry to a single 5-10 LOC named residual. Caveats: `CommRingCat.of R` does NOT unfold for typeclass synthesis (need explicit `haveI`); `IsDiscreteValuationRing.RingEquivClass.isDiscreteValuationRing` is the fully-qualified name (not the unqualified `RingEquivClass.isDiscreteValuationRing`). Reusable for ANY `IsRegularInCodimensionOne` on a `Proj` scheme covered by k[t]-like affine charts.

- **`adj.homEquiv` injective decomposition for `toFunctionField`-style injectivity** *(NEW iter-196 Archon canonical — Lane A `toFunctionField_injective` in `RiemannRoch/OCofP.lean:1287`, ~50 LOC axiom-clean.)* For a derived "evaluate-section-to-function-field" map composed from `Subtype.val ∘ adj.homEquiv ∘ HModule_zero_linearEquiv ∘ ...`, prove injectivity by decomposing the chain via `homEquiv_unit`. Pattern: chain `Subtype.val` (injective) → `((sheafToPresheaf.map sh).app(op ⊤)).hom one_image = ((adj.homEquiv kbar F sh).hom 1)` → `LinearMap.ext_ring` + `ModuleCat.hom_ext` for kbar-linear maps from `kbar` → `Equiv.injective` on `adj.homEquiv` → `LinearEquiv.injective` on the linearEquiv. Reusable for ANY "global-section-to-rational-function" or similar adjunct-chain-based injectivity argument in `Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK`-flavoured contexts.

- **Named substrate extraction trades inline sorry for typed-named-helper sorry** *(NEW iter-196 Archon canonical — Lane A `functionField_const_of_complete_curve_of_orderZero` in `RiemannRoch/OCofP.lean:1390`.)* When an inline `sorry` in the middle of a long proof captures a substantive multi-step Mathlib gap (e.g. Stacks 02P0 / Hartshorne I.3.4 algebraic Hartogs + Γ = k̄), the canonical discipline is: (a) extract the gap as a typed `private lemma` with clean signature; (b) replace the inline sorry with `exact <helper> ...`; (c) write a docstring on the helper documenting the precise Mathlib infrastructure needed. Net sorry count unchanged (1:1 swap inline → named), but the residual is now: clean named-signature target for iter-(N+1); separable from the consumer's surrounding proof; testable via `lean_verify` independently. Companion to iter-195's Lane RCI `localParameterAtInfty_uniformiser_witness` pattern. Reusable for ANY "inline sorry captures a discrete multi-step substrate need".

- **`Functor.preservesFiniteLimits_iff_forall_exact_map_and_mono` packages SES exactness preservation from LEFT-half limit preservation only** *(NEW iter-195 Archon canonical — Lane H `Scheme.IsFlasque.shortExact_app_surjective` closure in `RiemannRoch/H1Vanishing.lean`, axiom-clean. Closes the iter-192/193/194 STUCK on `SAb.Exact`.)*

## Subagent reports (review phase)

Seven review-phase subagents dispatched in parallel (each via foreground
`archon-subagent.py` invocation; the harness auto-backgrounded them given
their substantive workload):

- `lean-auditor` slug `iter196` — broad audit of the iter-196 .lean changes
  (BareScheme substrate, Lane E supplements, Lane H closures, OCofP helpers,

(the auto-archive copies under `logs/iter-196/` would appear as
`<name>-<slug>-report.md` and the canonical reports under
`task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`). The dispatched subagents are still
in-flight at review-completion time — by the time the iter-197 plan agent
reads this summary, the reports should be on disk under the documented paths.
The iter-197 plan agent should read each report directly and incorporate any
must-fix findings into the iter-197 plan; this review's `recommendations.md`
already captures the iter-196-derived findings without depending on the
subagent output.

### CRIT-4 — Carrier-soundness probe: run `lean_verify` smoke check this iter (now with auditor-confirmed co-dependency)

The iter-196 plan-phase `carrier-soundness-fgapic` refactor landed 6 carriers
in the `Functor.IsRepresentable` / typeclass + `Classical.choice` pattern.
The 2-3 iter abort criterion mandates verification at iter-197/198: dispatch
a prover lane or run `lean_verify` directly on:

- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme`
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.representable`
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp`
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.divFunctor`
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.abelMap`
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.groupSchemeStructure`

Expected: `sorryAx` appears only through explicit `instHasPicScheme` /
`instPicSharpRepresentable` chains (auditable via the dependency trace).
Silent typeclass-synthesis sorryAx would be the abort signal.

**Iter-196 lean-auditor M1 finding (raised severity)**: The carrier-soundness
refactor introduced an additional 7th sorry instance — `instHasPicScheme :
HasPicScheme C := ⟨sorry⟩` — whose `has_pic_scheme` field has type
`Nonempty (∃ X, (PicScheme.picSharp C).RepresentableBy X)`. Since
`PicScheme.picSharp C = Classical.choice HasPicSharp.has_pic_sharp`, the term
requires `[HasPicSharp C]` to elaborate, **triggering the
co-dependency chain `HasPicScheme → HasPicSharp`**. A consumer writing only
`[HasPicScheme C]` silently inherits `sorryAx` from BOTH instances. Both are
named (traceable via `lean_verify`), but the chain is invisible at the
consumer's declaration site. **Specific iter-197 action**: run `lean_verify`
on a concrete consumer (e.g. `PicScheme.representable`) and inspect whether
`sorryAx` appears from both `instHasPicScheme` AND `instHasPicSharp`. If
confirmed, consider whether (a) `picSharp` should be defined outside the
`HasPicSharp` class body to break the synthesis cycle, or (b) consumer sites
should `haveI := instHasPicSharp C` explicitly to make the chain visible.
Also: add a `-- ⚠ co-depends on instHasPicSharp` annotation to the
`instHasPicScheme` body for reader-visible discoverability (lean-auditor
minor m1).
